creator: jhb@FreeBSD.org
text:
In terms of the default hints for amd64: at this point ACPI is basically mandatory for amd64, and I think we should trim the default GENERIC hints to drop optional things like UARTs and LPT ports that are typically enumerated by ACPI anyway.  Or alternatively what we could do is say that we no longer add ISA devices by hints on amd64 and only use the hints for wiring purposes.  We could maybe change the "at" hint from "isa" to "acpi" for select devices so that the things are only used for setting unit numbers.

Adding Warner to see what he thinks about the hints for amd64.
